domain: Macrotermes termite mound (central chimney high thermal mass + peripheral flutes low thermal mass; diurnal temperature oscillation drives passive convection cell flushing CO2 without active pumping)
specific_mechanism: Two thermal-mass regimes (slow chimney + fast flutes) connected so external periodic forcing drives natural convection circulation that flushes metabolic byproducts.
llm_application: TERMITE-VENT: partition transformer parameters into two thermal-mass groups: SLOW-MASS group (deep layers, high parameter count) and FAST-MASS group (shallow layers, low parameter count). Apply a deliberately mis-phased two-channel LR schedule: slow-mass group gets a low-amplitude high-period cosine LR (chimney warming); fast-mass group gets a high-amplitude short-period cosine LR (flute warming). The phase offset between the two cycles drives passive 'gradient circulation' that flushes accumulated activation noise/drift each cycle without explicit buffer replay or active gating logic.
novelty_claim: Standard LR scheduling uses one global LR. Layer-wise LR (LARS/LAMB) scales by layer-norm. SGDR warm-restarts cycle LR globally. Discriminative fine-tuning (ULMFiT) uses one shared schedule. TERMITE-VENT differs by EXPLICIT two-thermal-mass parameter partition + DELIBERATELY PHASE-OFFSET two-channel LR cycles designed to drive passive convective gradient flush.
